column2:
affect = verb (mempengaruhi); effect = noun (efek/dampak)
"Affect" adalah kata kerja, "effect" adalah kata benda. Bahasa Indonesia tidak membedakan bentuk verb/noun jadi sering tertukar.
Climate change affects agriculture. (verb)
The effects of climate change are severe. (noun)

accept = menerima (verb); except = kecuali (preposition)
Pelajar Indonesia sering tertukar karena pengucapannya mirip. "Accept" = menerima sesuatu; "except" = pengecualian.
I accept your apology.
Everyone came except John.

advice = noun (nasihat); advise = verb (menasihati)
Bahasa Indonesia pakai satu kata "nasihat/menasihati" jadi mudah keliru. Ingat: "advice" dengan -ce = noun, "advise" dengan -se = verb.
She gave me good advice. (noun)
I advise you to study harder. (verb)

principal = kepala sekolah / utama; principle = prinsip/asas
Homofon yang sangat sering tertukar dalam writing. "PrincipAL" = orang/utama (think "pAL" = teman); "PrinciplE" = aturan/asas.
The school principal gave a speech.
Honesty is an important principle.

complement = melengkapi; compliment = pujian
Beda satu huruf, tapi makna jauh berbeda. "ComplEment" = melEngkapi (E for Enhance); "ComplIment" = pujIan (I for "I like it").
The wine complements the meal perfectly.
She gave me a nice compliment about my work.

stationAry = diam/tidak bergerak; stationEry = alat tulis
Homofon. Trik: stationEry = pEn/pEncil (alat tulis); stationAry = stAnding still (diam).
The car remained stationary at the traffic light.
I bought new stationery for school.

their = milik mereka; there = di sana; they're = they are
Homofon yang sering salah dalam writing. "Their" = posesif; "there" = tempat; "they're" = kontraksi.
Their house is big. (kepunyaan)
There are many books. / They're coming soon.

its = miliknya (posesif); it's = it is / it has
Aturan: kalau bisa diganti "it is" pakai "it's"; kalau tidak, pakai "its". Beda dengan posesif lain, "its" TIDAK pakai apostrof.
The dog wagged its tail. (posesif)
It's raining outside. (it is)

lose = kehilangan/kalah (verb); loose = longgar (adjective)
"Lose" satu O = verb; "loose" dua O = adjective. Pelafalan beda: lose /luːz/, loose /luːs/.
Don't lose your keys!
These pants are too loose.

than = daripada (perbandingan); then = kemudian/lalu (waktu)
"Than" untuk membandingkan; "then" untuk urutan waktu/konsekuensi.
She is taller than me. (perbandingan)
I finished work, then went home. (waktu)

fewer = countable nouns; less = uncountable nouns
Aturan IELTS penting! "Fewer" untuk yang bisa dihitung (people, books); "less" untuk yang tidak bisa dihitung (water, time, money).
Fewer students passed this year. (countable)
We have less time than expected. (uncountable)

amount = jumlah (uncountable); number = jumlah (countable)
Sama seperti less/fewer. "Amount of money/water"; "number of people/cars". Indonesian "jumlah" tidak membedakan.
A large amount of money was spent. (uncountable)
A large number of people attended. (countable)

between = di antara 2 hal; among = di antara 3+ hal
"Between" untuk dua benda spesifik; "among" untuk kelompok/lebih dari dua.
The secret is between you and me. (2 orang)
She is popular among her classmates. (banyak)

borrow = meminjam (mengambil); lend = meminjamkan (memberi)
Bahasa Indonesia "pinjam" sering ambigu. "I borrow FROM you" = saya ambil; "I lend TO you" = saya beri.
Can I borrow your pen? (saya yang ambil)
Can you lend me your pen? (kamu yang beri)

bring = membawa KE pembicara; take = membawa DARI pembicara
Arah perpindahannya berbeda. "Bring" = ke arah saya/kita; "take" = menjauh dari saya/kita.
Please bring your homework to class tomorrow. (ke sini)
Take this letter to the post office. (ke sana)

economic = berkaitan dengan ekonomi; economical = hemat/efisien
"Economic" untuk topik ekonomi; "economical" berarti tidak boros.
The country faces an economic crisis. (ekonomi)
This car is very economical on fuel. (hemat)

classic = klasik/terbaik dalam jenisnya; classical = berkaitan dengan musik/seni klasik atau zaman kuno
"Classic movie" = film legendaris; "classical music" = musik klasik (Mozart, Beethoven).
"Titanic" is a classic film. (terkenal/legendaris)
I love classical music. (genre Mozart, dll)

historic = bersejarah/penting; historical = berkaitan dengan sejarah
"Historic" = peristiwa penting yang akan dikenang; "historical" = apa pun yang berkaitan dengan masa lalu.
The moon landing was a historic event. (penting)
I enjoy reading historical novels. (bertema sejarah)

literally = harfiah/sungguhan; figuratively = kiasan
"Literally" sering disalahgunakan. Hanya pakai jika benar-benar terjadi secara harfiah.
I literally ran 10 km today. (benar-benar berlari)
I'm figuratively drowning in homework. (kiasan, tidak benar tenggelam)

famous = terkenal (positif/netral); notorious = terkenal karena hal buruk
Jangan bilang "Einstein is notorious" kecuali maksud negatif! "Notorious" selalu konotasi buruk.
Einstein is famous for his theory. (positif)
The criminal is notorious for his crimes. (negatif)

childlike = polos seperti anak (positif); childish = kekanak-kanakan (negatif)
Beda konotasi. "Childlike wonder" = positif; "childish behavior" = negatif (untuk orang dewasa).
She has a childlike sense of wonder. (positif, polos)
Stop being so childish! (negatif, tidak dewasa)

sensitive = sensitif/mudah tersinggung; sensible = bijaksana/masuk akal
False friend! "Sensible" BUKAN sensitif. "Sensible" = punya akal sehat.
He's very sensitive about his weight. (mudah tersinggung)
Wearing a coat in winter is sensible. (masuk akal)

Pakai "economic" (bukan "economical") dengan crisis, growth, policy, situation
Collocation tetap. "Economic" = adjective untuk topik ekonomi; jangan bilang "economical crisis" — salah!
The country is in an economic crisis.
The government's economic policy needs reform.

invent = menciptakan sesuatu yang baru; discover = menemukan sesuatu yang sudah ada
Edison "invented" the light bulb (ciptaan baru); Columbus "discovered" America (sudah ada, baru ditemukan).
Bell invented the telephone. (ciptaan baru)
Scientists discovered a new planet. (sudah ada)

column1: travel vs trip vs journey vs voyage
column2:
travel = verb umum; trip = perjalanan singkat; journey = perjalanan (proses); voyage = perjalanan laut/luar angkasa
"Travel" jarang sebagai countable noun. Pakai "trip" untuk perjalanan singkat dengan tujuan, "journey" untuk fokus prosesnya.
I love to travel. / We took a weekend trip to Bali.
The journey took 10 hours. / Columbus's voyage across the Atlantic.

wage = upah per jam/harian; salary = gaji bulanan/tahunan tetap
"Wage" biasanya untuk pekerja kasar/per jam; "salary" untuk profesional/tetap.
Factory workers earn an hourly wage.
Teachers receive a monthly salary.

job = pekerjaan tertentu (countable); work = pekerjaan secara umum (uncountable)
"I have a job" (✓), "I have a work" (✗). "Work" = aktivitas, "job" = posisi/role.
I have a new job as a teacher. (countable)
I have a lot of work to do. (uncountable)

learn = memperoleh ilmu/skill (hasil); study = proses belajar (aktivitas)
"Study" = melakukan kegiatan belajar; "learn" = mendapat pengetahuan dari proses itu.
I study English every day. (aktivitas)
I learned a lot from this book. (hasilnya tahu)

teach = mengajar (memberi); learn = belajar (menerima)
Indonesian speakers SANGAT sering bilang "She learned me English" — SALAH! Yang benar "She taught me English."
The teacher teaches us math. (guru → murid)
We learn math from the teacher. (murid menerima)

raise = transitive (butuh objek); rise = intransitive (tanpa objek)
"Raise something" = mengangkat sesuatu; "rise" = naik sendiri. Past: raised/raised vs rose/risen.
Please raise your hand. (butuh objek "hand")
The sun rises in the east. (tanpa objek)

lay = meletakkan (transitive, butuh objek); lie = berbaring (intransitive)
Past tense membingungkan: lay-laid-laid vs lie-lay-lain. "Lie" juga bisa berarti berbohong (lie-lied-lied).
Please lay the book on the table. (butuh objek)
I want to lie down. (tanpa objek)

sit = verb (duduk); seat = noun (kursi/tempat duduk) atau verb (mendudukkan)
"Sit down" (verb); "Take a seat" (noun). Jangan bilang "I seat" untuk "saya duduk".
Please sit down. (verb)
Is this seat taken? (noun)

column1: say vs tell vs talk vs speak
column2:
say = mengatakan (kata-kata); tell = memberitahu (orang); talk = mengobrol; speak = berbicara (formal/bahasa)
"Tell" butuh objek orang ("tell me"); "say" tidak ("say something"). "Speak" untuk bahasa atau formal.
She said hello. / She told me a story.
We talked for hours. / I speak English.

hear = mendengar (pasif, tidak sengaja); listen = mendengarkan (aktif, fokus)
"Hear" terjadi otomatis; "listen to" perlu usaha. "Listen" hampir selalu butuh "to".
I can hear the rain. (pasif)
I listen to music every day. (aktif, butuh "to")

see = melihat (pasif); look at = memandang (sengaja); watch = menonton (mengikuti gerakan)
"See" otomatis; "look at" sengaja sekilas; "watch" untuk sesuatu yang bergerak/berlangsung lama.
I saw a bird. / Look at this photo!
I watch TV every evening. (gerakan/durasi)

expect = mengira/memperkirakan akan terjadi; hope = berharap (ingin terjadi); wait = menunggu
"Expect" netral (prediksi); "hope" emosional (keinginan); "wait" untuk waktu. Indonesian "berharap" sering jadi salah jadi "wait".
I expect rain tomorrow. / I hope you pass!
I waited for the bus. (butuh "for")

remember = mengingat (sendiri); remind = mengingatkan (orang lain)
"I remember" = saya yang ingat; "remind me" = kamu yang membuat saya ingat.
I remember your birthday. (sendiri ingat)
Please remind me to call mom. (kamu mengingatkan)

column1: used to vs be used to vs get used to
column2:
used to + verb = dulu pernah; be used to + V-ing = sudah terbiasa; get used to + V-ing = mulai terbiasa
Tiga struktur sangat berbeda! Perhatikan tense dan bentuk verb setelahnya.
I used to smoke. (dulu, sudah berhenti)
I am used to waking up early. / I got used to the weather.

since = dari titik waktu spesifik; for = durasi/lamanya
"Since 2020" (titik mulai); "for 5 years" (lamanya). Keduanya pakai present perfect.
I have lived here since 2020. (titik waktu)
I have lived here for five years. (durasi)

during + noun (selama); while + clause (sementara, S+V); for + duration (selama berapa lama)
Sangat sering tertukar. "During the meeting" (noun); "while I was eating" (clause); "for 2 hours" (durasi).
I fell asleep during the movie. (+ noun)
While I was studying, he called. (+ clause) / I studied for 3 hours.
